meta.name: VibeCoach
meta.buildNumberSource: git rev-list --count HEAD (set by Build Phase script in project.pbxproj)
meta.versioningPolicy: Two version numbers (Epic #63 story 63.6). Build number (CFBundleVersion) auto-increments every commit via git rev-list --count HEAD (Build Phase). Marketing version (CFBundleShortVersionString, semver) is driven by release-please: each push to main updates a rolling Release PR; merging it cuts the vX.Y.Z tag + GitHub Release, and the Build Phase then stamps CFBundleShortVersionString from git describe --tags (fallback to the source Info.plist value for tag-less builds). appVersion below mirrors the current marketing version. docRevision is bumped on every architecture-relevant change (see CLAUDE.md §7.1). See ARCHITECTURE.md#11.
meta.summary: iOS app acting as a personal, proactive fitness coach. Combines HealthKit + Strava + AI (BYOK) to dynamically adjust training schedules per the Management-by-Exception principle.

principles.title: Management by Exception
principles.description: The app never alerts on good behaviour — only on deviations (training too hard or too light). A red status always comes with an AI-generated recovery plan.

principles.title: Privacy-first
principles.description: HealthKit data stays on-device. AI keys in Keychain. Strava client_secret in a Cloudflare Worker, never in the IPA.

principles.title: Type-safe domain model
principles.description: SwiftData with strict enums (SportCategory, EventFormat, TrainingPhase). Raw external data is mapped at the front door before persistence.

principles.title: Bring Your Own Key
principles.description: The user brings their own API key (Gemini / OpenAI / Anthropic). No Secrets fallback, no backend AI proxy.

principles.title: Proactive without an open app
principles.description: Two independent background triggers — Engine A reacts to action (new workout), Engine B to inaction. Fully local, no APNs in the proactive flow.

layers.name: App-bootstrap
layers.description: Entry-point, ModelContainer-init, AppDelegate, AppNavigationState. Routes between Onboarding and the tab host.

layers.name: Views (SwiftUI)
layers.description: Screens + components. Read state via @EnvironmentObject (ThemeManager, AppNavigationState, TrainingPlanManager) and query SwiftData via @Query.

layers.name: ViewModels & Formatters
layers.description: Pure-Swift context-builders and prompt-formatters that assemble the right blocks for the AI coach (blueprint, intent, history, vibe, preferences).

layers.name: Services
layers.description: Domain logic: HealthKit sync, Strava OAuth, physiological calculations, periodisation, pattern detection, weather. Pure-Swift where possible (testable without UI).

layers.name: Models (SwiftData @Model)
layers.description: Persistent domain types + schema snapshots (SchemaV1/V2/V3) + migration plan.

layers.name: External Systems
layers.description: Apple frameworks (HealthKit, BGTaskScheduler), Strava API via Cloudflare Worker proxy, Open-Meteo, and three AI providers.

modules.description: @main entry point. Builds the ModelContainer with AppMigrationPlan + a defensive fallback. Injects env objects (AppNavigationState, TrainingPlanManager, ThemeManager).
Registers BGTaskScheduler (Engine B handler) and is the UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate with the M-08 whitelist (only `goalRisk` and `recovery_plan` payloads).
Root router: chooses between OnboardingView and AppTabHostView based on `hasCompletedOnboarding`.
Main tab bar with 5 tabs (Dashboard, Goals, Coach, Memory, Settings). Manages the always-on dual-source sync via async let.
Main screen: Vibe Score, goal burndown, weekly calendar, dedupe + reclassify pipeline in scenePhase. Caches risk data in UserDefaults for Engine A/B.

Banner on the Dashboard that appears when MigrationFallbackStore has an active fallback date. Communicates that local-only data (FitnessGoal/UserPreference/Symptom) was rebuilt; HK + Strava workouts re-sync automatically. The dismiss button clears the flag.
Per-goal detail hub: blueprint milestones, periodisation phase, recovery-plan banner.
AI coach with coach cards (Epic 30). Blocked while there is no API key in the Keychain.

Per-workout deep dive: HR chart with pattern pins, chip row, secondary + cadence chart (running), scrubber card, 3-sentence Coach analysis (cached per workout). Fetches the hourly weather range + HK stepCount cadence fallback. Contains the manualSessionTypeOverride flow.
Settings: AI provider + key, theme picker, threshold settings, historical sync, source preference (HK/Strava).
4-page TabView carousel with permission buttons (HealthKit, notifications) and BYOK entry.

Orchestrates the AI call: builds the prompt blocks via the formatters, reads the key from the Keychain, streams the response. Test bypass via GenerativeModelProtocol. Story 61.7: 17 PHI context strings moved from cleartext UserDefaults to CoachContextCache (SwiftData, file-protected). Configured with modelContext via configure(with:) on first view appear.
Builds the per-workout context block for the coach prompt (Epic 45). Adds pattern detections from recent workouts.
Formats blueprint milestones (✅/❌ per critical workout) for the coach prompt (Epic 48).
Determines the active TrainingPhase (Base/Build/Peak/Taper) and the weekly TRIMP target with a phase multiplier (×1.00 / ×1.15 / ×1.30 / ×0.60).
Computes the daily Vibe Score (0-100) from HRV (50%, 7-day personal baseline) + sleep (50%, linear 5-8h).
Singleton that manages Engine A (HKObserverQuery) and Engine B (BGAppRefreshTask). Cooldown: at most 1 proactive notification per goal per 24h.
Gateway to HealthKit: HRV, sleep stages, workouts. enableBackgroundDelivery for Engine A.
Periodic + on-demand sync of HK workouts into ActivityRecord. Uses ActivityDeduplicator.smartInsert to prevent duplicate records.

Strava client. The central `ensureValidToken()` guard refreshes via the Cloudflare Worker; all endpoints (fetchLatestActivity, fetchActivityById, fetchActivityStreams, fetchRecentActivities, fetchHistoricalActivities) route through it.

OAuth2 with the Cloudflare Worker proxy. No client_secret in the IPA — only a shared X-Client-Token. CSRF protection via a random `state` UUID. Story 61.7: logout(modelContext:) clears both UserDefaults PHI keys and CoachContextCache (SwiftData) on disconnect.
Pure-Swift dedupe. `smartInsert` at the front door (HK + Strava ingest) and `runDedupe` as a scenePhase pass. Richness heuristic: samples > deviceWatts > trimp > avgHR > stable id tiebreaker.
Backfill of Strava `/streams` for recent records without samples. Writes via WorkoutSampleStore. 100ms throttle.
Read/write for fine-grained workout samples (5s buckets HR/power/cadence/speed/distance). FK = workoutUUID. Idempotent replaceSamples.
Determines the session type (Easy / Tempo / Threshold / Interval / VO2max) based on HR-zone distribution. Falls back to avg HR when samples are missing.
Reclassifies the session type for records that just got samples (after a Strava backfill). Protected by ActivityRecord.manualSessionTypeOverride.
Detects four patterns (aerobic decoupling, cardiac drift, cadence fade, slow HR recovery) on 5s-resampled samples. Gated on personal HR zones.

Epic #55 story 55.2: pure-Swift synthesis of multi-day event stage entries ('Etappe X/N') for the week schedule. Stage entries take precedence over coach trainings on event days (visual cross-goal suppression). Only events with resolvedEventDurationDays > 1 qualify.
Epic #56: pure-Swift, multilingual (NL/EN/DE/ES) extraction of (start, end) place names from a goal's free text ('van X naar Y'). Two-pass to avoid the greedy Spanish 'a' connector. Feeds CLGeocoder.
Epic #56: pure-Swift great-circle (slerp) interpolation of the approximate per-stage coordinate (day 1 = start, day N = end).
Epic #56: reusable Open-Meteo /v1/forecast daily fetch for arbitrary coordinates -> [DayForecast]. Owns the WMO->Dutch condition mapping (single source of truth; WeatherManager delegates).

Epic #56: @MainActor ObservableObject orchestrating location-aware per-stage weather for multi-day events: resolve route (RouteParser + CLGeocoder, cached), interpolate per stage, fetch forecast (OpenMeteoForecastClient), reverse-geocode place name. Exposes [Date: StageWeather] for the week schedule.
Pure-Swift, AppStorage-free. Detects contiguous samples with power<5 and cadence<5 for at least 45s. Peak-anchored 90s recovery window.
Generates a 3-sentence Coach analysis per workout with patterns + recovery events + blueprint context + periodization context + weather context. Gemini call, cached.
The cache key contains goalsFingerprint + weatherFingerprint so a milestone achievement, phase transition or later weather sync invalidates the cache.

Central purge of the remaining cleartext UserDefaults caches (latestSuggestedPlanData, latestCoachInsight, atRiskGoalTitles). Story 61.7: the 17 ChatViewModel PHI context strings moved to CoachContextCache (SwiftData); purgeSwiftData(from:) covers that side. Pure helper: UserDefaults injected (§6).

SwiftData @Model (singleton) storing the 17 PHI prompt-context strings that previously lived as cleartext in UserDefaults/@AppStorage. Inherits NSFileProtectionCompleteUnlessOpen from the container (Story 61.3). Loaded and created lazily via ChatViewModel.configure(with:). clearAll() resets on Strava disconnect.

Code-side safety bounds on model-proposed training plans (L-2/LLM09). Clamps suggestedDurationMinutes (≤600m) and targetTRIMP (≤1000, negatives→0) before persistence/display, independent of the prompt guardrails. Pure value-in/value-out (§6); runs at the single TrainingPlanManager.updatePlan chokepoint.

Sanitizes externally-sourced free text (e.g. Strava activity.name) before it enters the coach prompt (L-1/LLM01 prompt-injection): strips control chars/newlines, collapses whitespace, caps length, placeholder for blank. Pure (§6).

Single source of truth for coordinate privacy: rounds GPS/geocoded lat-lon to 0.1° (~11km) before any weather request, so the privacy margin is explicit and no longer depends on CLLocationManager.desiredAccuracy (review L-5). Pure (§6). Used by WeatherManager, OpenMeteoForecastClient and HistoricalWeatherService.

Epic #62 story 62.1 — pure-Swift, UI-free validation for the goal create/edit form: enforces a target date at least minimumLeadDays (7) out (earliestTargetDate/isTargetDateValid, Calendar-based §3), trims the title (sanitizedTitle), and classifies a stretch (target finish) time per sport against plausibleFinishRange (StretchTimePlausibility). Returns value types; the View owns the copy.

Epic #62 story 62.2 — pure-Swift hardening for the BYOK key field: sanitize() strips all whitespace/newlines from a paste, inferredProvider()/isProviderMismatch() detect a key whose prefix (sk-ant- / sk- / AIza) belongs to a different provider than selected. Never guesses on an unrecognised prefix (no Mistral false positives).

Epic #62 story 62.2 — persists the 'key works' verdict per provider so it survives a provider switch + app restart. Stores only a SHA256 fingerprint of the key (§11 privacy), never the key. UserDefaults-injected (§6).
Queries Open-Meteo (archive or forecast with past_days) for Strava records without iPhone presence. GPS rounded to 0.1° for privacy.
Realtime weather via Open-Meteo + CoreLocation for dashboard context.
Computes personal HR zones from max HR and lactateThresholdHR.
Computes Coggan power zones from FTP.
Manages storage/retrieval of LTHR + FTP. Feeds PauseDetector and WorkoutPatternDetector with referenceHR.
Pure-Swift estimator that derives LTHR/FTP from historical workout data when the user does not set them manually.
Detects the blueprint type (Marathon / Half / Cycling tour) via keywords or a SportCategory fallback. Marks essentialWorkouts as achieved/missed.
Penalty multiplier (1.0-1.4×) on TRIMP for a risky sport+injury combination.
Wrapper around kSecClassGenericPassword. Stores Strava tokens + BYOK API keys with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly (M-4: device-only, excluded from backups/restore).
Type-safe layer over KeychainService. Per-provider key slots (Epic #53) + the one-time migrations from UserDefaults and from the legacy single key (both idempotent).

Pure-Swift wrapper around the `vibecoach_migrationFallbackAt` UserDefaults flag. Written by AIFitnessCoachApp on a successful fresh-DB fallback (CLAUDE.md §12); read by MigrationFallbackBanner; cleared on user dismiss. AppStorage-free for testability.

Reads CFBundleShortVersionString + CFBundleVersion from Bundle.main and formats it as 'VibeCoach 2.0.0 (build 627)' for the Settings footer. Bundle injection via a parameter so tests can supply a fixture bundle.

Pure-Swift validator for the four personal training thresholds (Max HR, Resting HR, LTHR, FTP). Two kinds of checks: per-field range (warning on unusual, error on absurd) + cross-validation over the profile (Max HR > Resting HR, LTHR < Max HR, LTHR > Resting HR). Feeds ThresholdEditSheet live feedback and the zone-card explanation.

Explicit scope restriction that ChatViewModel prepends to the system prompt. Lists allowed coaching topics (training, recovery, injuries, goals, sports nutrition) + a fixed refusal framing for off-topic questions. Exception for sport-related 'off-topic' questions (e.g. 'can I train with this headache?').

Pure-Swift mapper from raw SDK/network errors to specific user messages. URLError codes for offline/timeout/DNS/cancelled; GenerateContentError cases via type-name matching (no circular SDK dep) for safety-block / invalid-key / overload; plus AIProviderError for the non-Gemini REST clients. Replaces the old generic 'temporary problem' message in ChatViewModel.

Pure-Swift helper that generates the banner text when the user switches model in Settings during an in-flight coach call. Compares the active-request snapshot (from ChatViewModel) with the current AppStorage model names and returns a specific message — a primary change takes precedence over a fallback change.

Generic pure-Swift split helper that splits a message array over 50 items into an archived front part (oldest) and a visible tail. Used by ChatView to collapse long conversations behind a 'Show earlier X messages' toggle. UI optimisation only — the chat API receives no message history.

Pure-Swift validator that clamps chat input to 5000 characters (maxLength) and only shows the counter from 4000 characters (80%). A paste that exceeds the limit is truncated and marked via didClamp so the view can show a one-time trim toast. Prevents 45s timeouts on large text blocks.

Pure-Swift parser that extracts the Retry-After time from a 429 response. Supports both delta-seconds (`60`) and an RFC 7231 IMF date (`Mon, 06 May 2024 13:03:20 GMT`). Default cooldown 15 min when the header is missing or malformed; dates in the past fall back to the default instead of an immediate retry to prevent retry storms.

UserDefaults-backed store for the Strava rate-limit cooldown. Writes the retry time as a Double so @AppStorage in SyncStatusBanner can bind to it reactively; `currentCooldown(now:)` clears expired entries automatically. Injected into FitnessDataService so a 429 is respected across app launches.

Lightweight @MainActor singleton wrapper around NWPathMonitor. Publishes `isOnline` (Bool) and mirrors the offline status to UserDefaults so non-View-layer callers (the SyncStatusStore snapshot) have a synchronous read path. Idempotent `start()`; started from AIFitnessCoachApp.init so the banner has the correct state right from launch.

Pure-Swift, UserDefaults-backed store with per-source last-success timestamps and last-error categories (network/auth/rateLimit/decoding/other). Filters out FitnessDataError.missingToken — users without a Strava connection get no banner. Provides a SyncStatusSnapshot for SyncBannerStateBuilder. Writes Double timestamps (Unix epoch) so @AppStorage in the View can read reactively.

Pure function that determines the right banner state from a SyncStatusSnapshot. Strict priority offline > rate-limited > error > nil; with two error sources the most recent wins. A leftover `.rateLimit` category is ignored once the cooldown has expired so the banner does not stay up permanently.

Dashboard banner that consumes SyncBannerStateBuilder and — depending on the state — shows an offline message (with last-sync time), a rate-limit message (resumes at HH:MM) or an error message (Strava/HealthKit, dismissable). Reactive via @ObservedObject on NetworkReachabilityMonitor + @AppStorage bindings on the SyncStatusStore keys.
Strava OAuth token storage in the Keychain.
Centralised os.Logger instances per category. Privacy modifiers mandatory for user data (HRV, RPE, tokens, UUIDs).
Manages the active theme (moss/stone/mist/clay/sakura/ink), saturation and SF Symbol iconography. Adaptive to light/dark.
Single Source of Truth for the active 7-day schedule. Consumed by Dashboard, Goals and Coach.
Gated on -UITesting + #if DEBUG. Writes a dummy API key, weather data, periodisation context. UITestMockGenerativeModel replaces the Gemini call.
@Model for a workout — HK uuid or Strava id, sport, TRIMP, avgHR, deviceWatts, temperatureCelsius, humidityPercent, manualSessionTypeOverride.
@Model for a 5s-bucket sample. Key: workoutUUID. One ActivityRecord ↔ N samples (no SwiftData relationship, a filtered fetch).
Training goal: title, targetDate, blueprint, sportCategory, recoveryPlanActiveSince.
Per-day Vibe Score cache: hrvScore, sleepScore, totalScore.
Athletic settings: maxHR, lactateThresholdHR, FTP, weight.
Smart Expiring Memory: preferred sport, available days, preferences with an optional expiry date.
Injury/complaint with a bodyArea enum and an expiry date. Local-only — not re-syncable.
SchemaMigrationPlan with staged stages between SchemaV1 → V2 → V3. Type changes via willMigrate/didMigrate.
Frozen snapshot of the old schema. Nested @Model types keep the same unqualified name.
Intermediate version. Contains the first type-safe enums (SportCategory).
Current schema version. `models` is used by AIFitnessCoachApp.makeModelContainer() as the source of truth.
Apple HealthKit: HRV (heartRateVariabilitySDNN), sleepAnalysis (incl. stages), workouts. Engine A runs on HKObserverQuery + enableBackgroundDelivery.
iOS BackgroundTasks. For Engine B: BGAppRefreshTask registered in AppDelegate before return true.
Local notification scheduling. No APNs in the proactive flow.
Cloudflare Worker (https://github.com/markclausing/vibecoach-proxy). Endpoints `/oauth/strava/exchange` and `/oauth/strava/refresh`. Strava client_secret as a Worker Secret.
Strava REST API — activities + streams (HR, power, cadence, speed, distance). All calls via FitnessDataService.ensureValidToken().
Free weather API. `api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast` (realtime + past_days) + `archive-api.open-meteo.com` (>5 days old).
Gemini (default `gemini-flash-latest`), OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Mistral. BYOK via UserAPIKeyStore. No backend proxy — calls go directly from the device to the provider.

Provider-agnostic client factory (Epic #53, Story 61.8). Routes per AIProvider to GeminiRestClient (Gemini v1beta REST, replaces generative-ai-swift SDK), OpenAICompatibleModelClient (OpenAI + Mistral, /v1/chat/completions) or AnthropicModelClient (/v1/messages). Encapsulates system-instruction placement, JSON mode and HTTP error mapping (AIProviderHTTP.validate → AIProviderError).

Story 61.8 — pure URLSession REST client for Gemini API v1beta /generateContent. Replaces the deprecated generative-ai-swift SDK (CWE-1104). Implements GenerativeModelProtocol + RealAIProviderClient. Auth: x-goog-api-key header (key stays out of URL/access logs). JSON mode: generationConfig.response_mime_type. Image: inline_data parts. Custom validateGemini() maps HTTP 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT to AIProviderError.authenticationFailed.
Epic #54 — fetches /v1/models live per provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Mistral) directly with the BYOK key, filters to chat models, falls back to AIModelCatalog.builtIn. Gemini stays on the Worker.

Single source of truth for the app language (NL/EN/DE/ES). Resolves the active Locale for both SwiftUI (.environment(\.locale)) and pure-Swift readers (currentLocale / currentPromptLanguageName), backed by @AppStorage 'vibecoach_appLanguage'. .system follows the device; a specific choice writes an AppleLanguages bundle override (applied on next launch). promptLanguageName drives the coach's 'respond in {language}' directive.

Epic #55 story 55.3: pure-Swift formatter that injects an [EVENT WINDOW] block per multi-day event — no other training / fixed preferences in the window, cross-goal suppression, and post-event recovery. Cached via ChatViewModel.cacheEventWindow.

Gap analysis and per-phase timeline for goals. BlueprintGap calculates the cumulative TRIMP/km shortfall within the current phase; analyzeGaps returns at-risk goals. Epic #60: phaseTimeline(for:activities:now:) builds all four phases with targets (longest session + weekly TRIMP, taper inversion) and buckets blueprint milestones per phase. Also contains TRIMPTranslator (TRIMP → readable duration/distance).

Epic #60 computed value-types (no @Model, no migration): PhaseTimeline/PhaseSummary/PhaseTarget/PhaseMilestone/PhaseWindow/PhaseStatus. Describe each training phase per goal with date range, status (past/current/future), targets and milestones for the expandable Goals view.

Epic #60 single source of truth for phase windows (week budget: taper 2 / peak 2 / build ≤8 / base = rest, anchored on targetDate). Feeds both the phase bar (GoalsListView.phaseSegments) and the per-phase milestone list, so they no longer diverge. Pure-Swift, AppStorage-free.

Epic #60 expandable per-phase milestones in the Goals view: a DisclosureGroup per phase (current phase open by default). Collapsed = phase colour dot + name + date range + status; expanded = target rows with progress bar (taper '(max)') + milestone rows with due date + satisfied checkmark. Replaces the old four-point timeline.

Builds the 'last workout' block for the coach context: RPE category (light/moderate/hard/maximal), TRIMP, session type intent. Epic #57: readableMood(_:) maps the mood SF Symbol name to a readable English word (good/strong/exhausted/in pain/calm). Pure-Swift, AppStorage-free.

subsystems.title: Dual Engine Architecture
subsystems.description: Two independent background triggers — Engine A reacts to new HK workouts, Engine B to prolonged inactivity with a red goal.

subsystems.title: Strava OAuth via Cloudflare Worker
subsystems.description: client_secret moved from Secrets.swift to a Worker Secret. CSRF protection via a random state UUID.

subsystems.title: BYOK — API Keys in the Keychain
subsystems.description: No Secrets fallback. The migration from UserDefaults is idempotent. Per-provider key slots since Epic #53.

subsystems.title: Dual-source Sample Pipeline
subsystems.description: WorkoutSample with a workoutUUID FK (deterministically derived for Strava). Smart-ingest at the front door + scenePhase pass (backfill → dedupe → reclassify).

subsystems.title: Workout Pattern Detection
subsystems.description: Four patterns on 5s-resampled samples, gated on personal HR zones. HR recovery is pause-based (peak-anchored 90s window).

subsystems.title: Coach-prompt Context Injection
subsystems.description: ChatViewModel composes the prompt from separate formatter blocks; WorkoutInsightService has its own context pipeline with a cache fingerprint on goals + weather.

subsystems.title: CI Pipeline (Epic 46)
subsystems.description: GitHub Actions: SwiftLint + Unit Tests + UI Tests (sequential) + Coverage Report DAG. Plus CodeQL for Swift + Actions.

subsystems.title: Localization & multilingual coach (Epic #37)
subsystems.description: UI + AI coach in NL/EN/DE/ES. UI strings in Localizable.xcstrings (~527 keys); shared components render via Text(LocalizedStringKey(var)), computed props via String(localized:). The English coach prompt steers output language via a locale-dependent 'respond in {language}' directive (no per-language prompt copies). Prompt-interpolated enum values stay Dutch as the prompt term and are localized only at the View render site (UI-vs-prompt split). Detection logic (injuryKeywords, day-name parsing, rest/sport classification) is language-independent.

steps.action: Init ModelContainer with AppMigrationPlan; fallback on migration error
Register the BGTaskScheduler handler before return true
If onboarded: setupEngineA() + scheduleEngineB()
Route to OnboardingView or AppTabHostView
Trigger TriggerAutoSync (HK + Strava concurrent via async let)
HKObserverQuery wakes the app via enableBackgroundDelivery
Check whether a goal is in the red (cached in UserDefaults)
If yes + cooldown OK: schedule a local push (type=goalRisk)
Show banner; deep-link to the Goals tab
Wake the app at the scheduled moment
Check: >2 days inactive and a red goal?
Schedule an empathetic motivation notification
Fetch new HK workouts; map to ActivityRecord
Fetch Strava activities via ensureValidToken()
smartInsert: source-id idempotent → ±5s cross-source check → insert
Backfill /streams for records without samples
scenePhase: runDedupe on the last 10 records
Reclassify records that just got samples
Fetch samples for workoutUUID
Resample into 5s buckets
Detect 4 patterns (gated on HR zones)
Find ≥45s pauses for recovery events
Build context + check cache (goalsFingerprint + weatherFingerprint)
Gemini call for the 3-sentence Coach analysis
Cache the result per workout

flows.name: Chat with AI Coach
flows.trigger: User sends a message in ChatView

steps.action: Read the API key from the Keychain (UserAPIKeyStore)
[VIBE SCORE] block
[BLUEPRINT] block with milestones
[PREFERENCES] block
[RECENT WORKOUTS] block with patterns
Stream the response from the chosen provider
Build the authorize URL with a random state UUID (CSRF)
User authorizes; redirect with code + state
Validate state against the callback URL
POST /oauth/strava/exchange (X-Client-Token header)
Store tokens in the Keychain

flows.name: BYOK Migration (one-time)
flows.trigger: First launch after the C-02 update

steps.action: init() calls UserAPIKeyStore.migrateFromUserDefaultsIfNeeded()
Read the legacy UserDefaults key; write to the Keychain; clear the UserDefaults entry

flows.name: SwiftData Schema Migration
flows.trigger: App launch with an old DB version

steps.action: ModelContainer(for: SchemaV3, migrationPlan: AppMigrationPlan)
Execute staged stages V1→V2→V3
On failure: log + delete the corrupt store + fresh V3 + UserDefaults flag for a UI message

flows.name: Release & Versioning (Epic #63)
flows.trigger: Push to main (continuous) + maintainer merging the Release PR (cut)

steps.action: Squash & merge a feature PR with a Conventional-Commit title (feat: / fix: / …)
On push to main: recompute the next semver from commit titles since the last tag; create/update the rolling Release PR 'chore(main): release X.Y.Z' + changelog
When ready to ship: merge the Release PR — this is the only moment the marketing version moves
Create git tag vX.Y.Z + GitHub Release + update .release-please-manifest.json
Subsequent builds stamp CFBundleShortVersionString from git describe --tags (fallback: source Info.plist). CFBundleVersion keeps auto-incrementing per commit independently.

styleGuide.language: Dutch for UI/prompts/comments; English for Swift identifiers
styleGuide.philosophy: Serene Visual Overhaul (Epic 29) — soft gradients, hierarchical SF Symbols, fixed 6-theme palette

testing.summary: Pure-Swift logic in Services/Models/ViewModels helpers gets unit-test coverage; happy-path UI flows via XCUITest. Deliberately not everything: trivial getters, view-layer orchestration without an injection seam, and simulator-sandbox-unreachable frameworks remain untested.

policy.title: What TO test
items: Pure-Swift logic in Services/, Models/, ViewModels/ helpers (classifiers, calculators, formatters, schedulers)
Schema migrations with file-backed seed stores (in-memory does not work for migration paths)
Domain rules with edge cases: HR-zone boundaries, injury-keyword detection, DST transitions, dedupe heuristics
Happy-path UI flows via XCUITest: onboarding, navigation to each tab, creating a goal

policy.title: What NOT to test
items: View-layer orchestration without a clean injection seam (concurrent sync, async let flows)
iOS framework things that do not work in the simulator sandbox (Keychain entitlements, BGTaskScheduler timing)

policy.title: Safeguarding testability
items: Keep pure-Swift helpers free of AppStorage/UserDefaults — caller injects state via parameters
Mocking for UI tests via UITestMockEnvironment.setup() (gated on -UITesting + #if DEBUG)
Test-only bypasses in production code only behind a -UITesting check + comment

suites.name: Unit Tests (XCTest)
suites.description: Pure-Swift tests with file-backed SwiftData stores where needed. No network — all external calls replaced by MockNetworkSession / MockGenerativeModel / MockTokenStore.
highlights: ActivityDeduplicatorTests — smart-ingest 3-layer check (source-id, ±5s cross-source, richness heuristic)
PauseDetectorTests — peak-anchored 90s window, minimumPauseSeconds=45, 10-active-samples pre-check
WorkoutPatternDetectorTests — HR-zone-gated decoupling, drift, cadence fade, recovery
SchemaMigrationV1ToV2Tests + V2ToV3Tests — file-backed seed stores as migration safety net
CalendarDSTSafeTests — date math without TimeInterval bugs across daylight saving
KeychainServiceTests — round-trip + service-name isolation
ProactiveNotificationServiceTests — Engine A/B cooldown logic

suites.name: UI Tests (XCUITest)
suites.description: Sequential on CI to avoid xctrunner-clone flakiness (Epic 46.4). UITestMockEnvironment injects a dummy API key + weather data + UITestMockGenerativeModel so views render without live API calls.
highlights: AIFitnessCoachUITests — TabBar navigation + Dashboard happy path
AIFitnessCoachUITestsLaunchTests — launch screenshot for the App Store
AIModelPickerUITests — E2E coverage of the model picker (launched via -UITestOpenAICoachConfig shortcut)
GoalsUITests — create / edit / delete a goal
OnboardingE2ETests — 4-page TabView flow with permission buttons

suites.name: Schema Migration Tests
suites.description: Mandatory for every @Model change (CLAUDE.md §2.1). Tests at minimum (1) that FitnessGoal/UserPreference survive the migration and (2) that new fields are writable after migration.
highlights: SchemaMigrationV1ToV2Tests
SchemaMigrationV2ToV3Tests

infrastructure.purpose: Injection seam for the AI call. Production delivers Gemini/OpenAI/Anthropic; tests deliver MockGenerativeModel or UITestMockGenerativeModel.
Unit-test mock — programmed responses per prompt.
Replaces KeychainService in tests that touch the OAuth flow.
URLSession stub — programmable per host/path. Prevents network I/O in unit tests.
Bootstrap for XCUITest: writes a dummy API key to Keychain, fake weather + periodisation context, and swaps the generative model. Gated on -UITesting + #if DEBUG.

coverage.summary: 61% combined coverage on testable code. Coverage is signal, not a KPI — `Views/` stays limited by SwiftUI testability and is reported separately.
coverage.tool: scripts/coverage-report.py (max-merge per file over unit-tests + ui-tests xcresults)

lessonsLearned.title: Run UI tests sequentially on CI
lessonsLearned.body: Parallel clones trigger `ipc/mig server died` on GitHub macos-latest runners (Epic 46.4 root cause). `-parallel-testing-enabled NO` on CI; locally the scheme config stays parallel for speed.

lessonsLearned.title: Inspect the xcresult first, then blame the runner
lessonsLearned.body: `xcrun xcresulttool get test-results activities --test-id <id>` often reveals test-code bugs (hidden NavigationBar, too-short timeouts) as the cause — not the runner.

lessonsLearned.title: In-memory stores do not work for migration tests
lessonsLearned.body: The migration plan requires a V<N> store file to start from. A file-backed seed store is mandatory in SchemaMigration*Tests.

ci.summary: GitHub Actions runs three workflows: `iOS CI` (4-job DAG) and `CodeQL Security Analysis` (Swift + Actions matrix) on every push to `main` and every PR, plus `release-please` (Epic #63) on push to `main` that maintains a Release PR and cuts semver tags + GitHub Releases from Conventional Commits. A concurrency group per branch cancels older in-flight runs on force-push.

workflows.name: iOS CI
workflows.description: Lint + Unit Tests + UI Tests + Coverage Report as a DAG. Coverage only runs when both test jobs are done (or when only unit tests pass — the UI bundle is then optional).

workflows.name: CodeQL Security Analysis
workflows.runner: matrix (macos-latest for swift, ubuntu-latest for actions)
workflows.description: Two parallel matrix cells: Swift on macOS with manual xcodebuild, Actions language on Ubuntu (10× cheaper) for YAML static analysis.

workflows.name: release-please
workflows.description: Epic #63 story 63.6 — maintains a rolling Release PR ('chore(main): release X.Y.Z') from Conventional-Commit titles; merging it cuts the vX.Y.Z tag + GitHub Release + changelog. Scoped contents:write + pull-requests:write so the test pipeline stays least-privilege. Config: release-please-config.json (release-type simple) + .release-please-manifest.json. The app build stamps CFBundleShortVersionString from the latest tag. See ARCHITECTURE.md#11.

jobs.name: SwiftLint
jobs.description: swiftlint --strict on .swiftlint.yml. 938→0 violations baseline; 1 new violation breaks CI. github-actions-logging reporter gives inline annotations on the PR diff.

jobs.name: Unit Tests
jobs.description: xcodebuild test -only-testing:AIFitnessCoachTests -enableCodeCoverage YES. xcresult bundle as artifact (7d retention).

jobs.name: UI Tests
jobs.description: Sequential (-parallel-testing-enabled NO) to avoid xctrunner-clone crashes. Boot simulator + check bootstatus before xcodebuild for SpringBoard warmup. xcresult + CoreSimulator logs as artifacts (14d retention).

jobs.name: Coverage Report
jobs.description: Merges both xcresults per-file (max-coverage) via scripts/coverage-report.py. Generates per-directory markdown with aggregates (Testable / Views / Total). Also runs when the UI-tests job is red — single-bundle mode then.

jobs.name: CodeQL Swift
jobs.description: Manual `xcodebuild clean build` + codeql-action analyze. Build mode `manual` because autobuild fails on SwiftData projects.

jobs.name: CodeQL Actions
jobs.description: Pure YAML static analysis on `.github/workflows/*.yml`. Detects untrusted-input injection, unpinned actions, secret leakage. No build needed — 10× cheaper than a macOS runner.

envSetup.detail: `cp AIFitnessCoach/Secrets-template.swift AIFitnessCoach/Secrets.swift` — so the project compiles without real keys.
`xcrun simctl list devices available | grep iPhone | ...` — no hardcoded model names, works across Xcode updates.
Run-Script Build Phase sets CFBundleVersion to `git rev-list --count HEAD` — locally and in CI, no manual bumps.

ci.concurrency: concurrency: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }} with cancel-in-progress: true on both workflows. Force-pushes on a PR branch cancel older in-flight runs.

agent.summary: VibeCoach is co-developed by an AI assistant (Claude Code, Opus 4.7). The agent follows the policy in CLAUDE.md, uses a persistent memory system for user context, and maintains the documentation discipline (§7) so that architecture, roadmap, and this viewer evolve with the code.

system.philosophy: Proactive doc updates without asking permission; summarise + cache-friendly work; clean up stale rules at Epic transitions.

principles.title: Management by Exception (for the agent too)
principles.description: The agent only alerts or asks on deviations — not on every trivial choice. Actions with blast radius (destructive git, force-push, shared infra) do get explicit confirmation.

principles.title: Autonomous context management
principles.description: On a new architecture choice, a structurally fixed bug, or a new project standard: update the right doc file directly without asking permission. See CLAUDE.md §0.

principles.title: One fix per PR
principles.description: No piggybacked refactors. Always link the source (CodeQL alert, issue, crash report). Add a regression test where feasible.

principles.title: Dutch, unless stated otherwise
principles.description: Replies + comments in Dutch. Code identifiers in English (Swift convention). Concise — no unnecessary summaries.

rules.title: SwiftData Strictness + Migration Protocol
rules.summary: Type-safe enums, no raw strings. Every @Model change requires a schema snapshot + MigrationStage + file-backed migration test — including pure additions.
highlights: Snapshot old schema in Models/SchemaV<N>.swift; nested @Model types keep the same unqualified name
For pure additions: MigrationStage.lightweight(from:to:) is sufficient
Type changes (String → enum): willMigrate + didMigrate capture/restore
Bump container-init in AIFitnessCoachApp.makeModelContainer() to the new SchemaV<M>.models
Mandatory test: FitnessGoal + UserPreference survive migration + new fields writable

rules.title: Time & Date
rules.summary: Never use TimeInterval math for periods. Always use Calendar.current.date(byAdding:to:). Base-building calculates from Date(), not targetDate.
highlights: TimeInterval × 86400 → bugs with daylight saving and leap years
Calendar works correctly across DST boundaries

rules.title: Dual Engine Architecture
rules.summary: Engine A (HKObserverQuery) reacts to action, Engine B (BGAppRefreshTask) to inaction. ProactiveNotificationService is the singleton for both.
highlights: Cooldown: max 1 proactive notification per goal per 24h
Risk data cached in UserDefaults from DashboardView (onAppear + after refresh)

rules.title: SwiftUI & iOS Style Rules
rules.summary: One top-level type per file. Soft cap ±500 LOC per Swift file. Standard iOS components — simple, modern, native.
highlights: For @Model classes, one-per-file is mandatory
Complex logic explained via comments in English
Pure file splits without semantic changes are always safe

rules.title: Testing Policy
rules.summary: Pure-Swift logic + edge cases + happy-path UI. NOT: trivial getters, view orchestration without an injection seam, simulator-unreachable frameworks.
highlights: Keep pure-Swift helpers free of AppStorage/UserDefaults
Run UI tests sequentially on CI (-parallel-testing-enabled NO)
Coverage is signal, not a KPI
On UI-test failures, inspect xcresult first

rules.title: Documentation Discipline
rules.summary: Four source-of-truth files (README, ROADMAP, ARCHITECTURE, CLAUDE) + two derived artefacts (architecture.html + .json). No overlap, no duplication.
highlights: Every feature PR updates relevant docs in the same commit
ROADMAP statuses: ✅ ⟶ 🔄 ⟶ ⏳
Pure docs-only PRs may go directly to main
Architecture viewer is kept in sync on every module-layer change (§7 Architecture visualisation)

rules.title: Git Workflow
rules.summary: Branch + PR — never directly on main. Branch-name conventions per type. Assistant creates the PR right after the first push; does not merge.
highlights: feature/epic-{nr}-..., fix/..., hotfix/..., security/..., ci/..., chore/..., docs/...
One fix per PR, no piggybacked refactors
Squash & merge — done by the user, not the agent
Never --no-verify or force-push to main

rules.title: Xcode Project Management
rules.summary: project.pbxproj has skip-worktree set. New Swift files require manual addition to PBXFileReference + BuildFile + PBXGroup + PBXSourcesBuildPhase.
highlights: Skip this step: CI build fails with 'cannot find X in scope'
Temporarily disable skip-worktree, stage, commit, re-enable

rules.title: Logger & Privacy
rules.summary: No print() in Services/Models/ViewModels. AppLoggers.<category>.<level>(...) with explicit privacy modifiers for user data.
highlights: privacy: .private — HRV, sleep, TRIMP, age, goal titles, UUIDs, tokens, RPE/mood
privacy: .public — framework error codes, counters, non-identifying status flags
No loose static let logger per service — centralise in AppLoggers

rules.title: Defensive App Init
rules.summary: On the critical launch path: never fatalError as the first catch. Try → log + fallback → only on second failure fatalError.
highlights: On migration failure: remove corrupt SQLite store + WAL/SHM sidecars
UserDefaults flag (vibecoach_migrationFallbackAt) as hook for a UI message
HK + Strava data are re-syncable — data-loss risk is acceptable over a bricked app

items.use: Tech debt, refactors without behaviour change

workflows.title: PR flow per branch
steps: Assistant creates PR immediately via `gh pr create` (title from branch name, body with Summary + Test plan)
User pulls and tests (feature branches on device)
Feedback → fixes on the same branch (push updates the existing PR)
Next sprint: delete merged branches locally + remote

workflows.title: Doc update protocol per PR type

lessonsLearned.incident: Two optional fields added to ActivityRecord without a schema bump. SwiftData's lightweight inference behaves differently with an explicit migrationPlan — container-init failed; fallback wiped FitnessGoal + UserPreference (local-only).
lessonsLearned.rule: Mandatory for every @Model change — including pure additions — a SchemaV<N> snapshot + MigrationStage. No more exceptions.

lessonsLearned.incident: XCUITest failed flakily on GitHub macos-latest with `ipc/mig server died`. Root cause: parallel xctrunner clones ('Clone 2 of iPhone 16 Pro') crash under load.
lessonsLearned.rule: Run UI tests sequentially on CI (`-parallel-testing-enabled NO`). On failures, inspect the xcresult activity log first before blaming runner flakiness — more often it is test-code bugs (hidden NavBar, too-short timeouts).

lessonsLearned.incident: Global peak + measure HR exactly 60s later. On continuous rides the user was already pedalling 60s after the peak, so a visual dip of 40 BPM during a short stop came out as a '4 BPM drop' from the detector.
lessonsLearned.rule: Measure HR recovery only during detected pauses (power<5 + cadence<5 for ≥45s). Peak-anchored 90s window instead of 60s from pause start — the vagal HRR phase runs to ~90s; an abrupt stop peaks 5-15s after the stop before it starts dropping.

lessonsLearned.incident: Three duplicate `static let logger = Logger(...)` declarations in different services got out of hand — different subsystem strings, some without privacy modifiers.
lessonsLearned.rule: Centralise in AppLoggers — one logger per category, privacy modifiers mandatory for user data. No static let logger per service.

lessonsLearned.incident: Strava client_secret was hardcoded in Secrets.swift — extractable from the IPA by any user who could open the binary.
lessonsLearned.rule: OAuth flow via a Cloudflare Worker proxy with the secret as a Worker Secret. Client authenticates with a shared X-Client-Token + random state UUID (CSRF).

lessonsLearned.incident: A leaner HK record regularly overwrote a richer Strava record with deviceWatts when syncs arrived in the wrong order. A manual 'Remove Duplicate Activities' button in Settings was the workaround.
lessonsLearned.rule: Smart-ingest at the front door: three-layer check (source-id idempotent → ±5s cross-source comparison → regular insert) at all ingest paths. Manual button removed — auto-dedupe + smart-ingest cover both sides.

lessonsLearned.incident: An APNs payload with an arbitrary `activityId` could navigate the app — a potential social-engineering vector.
lessonsLearned.rule: Whitelist in AppDelegate.isAllowedNotificationPayload(_:) — only `goalRisk` and `recovery_plan` payloads. All others silently ignored, in both willPresent and didReceive.

lessonsLearned.incident: ModelContainer-init with a migration plan could fail and brick the app until reinstall. A crash loop because fatalError was the first catch.
lessonsLearned.rule: On the critical launch path: try → log + fallback (remove corrupt store) → only on second failure fatalError. HK + Strava data are re-syncable; data-loss risk is acceptable over a bricked app.

tags.security-c01: Strava client_secret moved to Cloudflare Worker
tags.security-c02: AI API key moved from UserDefaults to Keychain
tags.security-h01: CSRF state UUID on OAuth callback
tags.epic-11: TrainingPlanManager as Single Source of Truth
tags.epic-48: Coach analysis linked to goals + periodisation
tags.epic-49: HK weather metadata in coach analysis
tags.epic-50: Open-Meteo historical weather for Garmin-only rides
tags.epic-51: User-feedback hardening — error messages, validation & visibility
